_note: Pre-reviewed golden for the France oral-nicotine ban, served when DEMO_MODE=offline or no ANTHROPIC_API_KEY. The presenter says 'offline mode' out loud. Status is IN FORCE (not 'proposed'). Line items run through the same citation rail as a live call; the last two are deliberate abstentions (France pouch revenue + XQS France share are not published).
narrative: France's oral-nicotine ban (Décret 2025-898) has been in force since April 2026 — a total ban, with a Conseil d'État merits ruling expected H2 2026. The direct EBITDA hit is modest: pouches are only ~5% of STG's group (NGP net sales were DKK 428m in FY2025) and France was never an XQS focus market. The sharper cost is strategic — with no compliant oral-nicotine product, the foreclosed France pouch revenue is gone, not deferred, removing a market from STG's stated DKK 1bn+ pouch ambition. France's pouch revenue is not published by STG, so it is an editable assumption here, not STG's own figure; the French machine-rolled cigar core is a separate exposure this ban does not touch.
